seoTitle: Connect QuickBooks to Wix and Sync Sales Data
metaDescription: Integrate your Wix store with QuickBooks Online to automatically sync orders, item mapping, and sales tax instead of manual entry.
intro: Manually copying Wix orders into your books invites errors and wastes hours. This guide shows how to connect QuickBooks Online to your Wix storefront through the Intuit App Marketplace so sales data flows in automatically.

steps.heading: Open the Apps marketplace
steps.body: In QuickBooks Online, go to the left sidebar and select Apps, then search for Wix in the marketplace.

steps.heading: Start the connection
steps.body: Click Get App Now to launch the sync wizard and accept the privacy consent to be redirected to Wix.

steps.heading: Authorize access
steps.body: Log into your Wix storefront credentials to grant secure read-write API access between the two platforms.

steps.heading: Map items and sales
steps.body: Match your Wix location profile to a checking account, then link store orders to the correct revenue accounts in your Chart of Accounts.

steps.heading: Set tax mapping and sync
steps.body: Configure how localized Wix tax lines map to your QuickBooks tax agencies, choose a historical data window, then save and sync.

faq.question: Will connecting Wix to QuickBooks import old orders?
faq.answer: Yes, you can choose a historical data retrieval window during setup to pull past orders into your books.

faq.question: Do I need to manually enter sales tax for Wix orders?
faq.answer: No, once mapped correctly, the integration routes localized Wix tax lines to the matching QuickBooks tax agencies automatically.

faq.question: How do I verify the sync is working?
faq.answer: Check your QBO transaction ledger to confirm new Wix orders and payout rows appear and match your storefront records.

faq.question: Can I map different Wix locations to different accounts?
faq.answer: Yes, the mapping step lets you associate specific Wix profiles with corresponding checking or revenue accounts.
